title: zornia dichotoma
intro: Zornia dichotoma, also known as White Vine or White Vine Climber, is a perennial legume native to the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. Traditionally, Zornia dichotoma has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves or seeds in hot water, and have been used to support overall health and well-being.
